Darrell Long.content: Give a bullet point biography of Darrell Long highlighting their contributions and achievements as a computer scientist, with each fact separated with a new line character.

Here's a bullet point biography of Darrell Long:

* Darrell Long is a renowned computer scientist who has made significant contributions to the field of data storage and computer systems.

* He received his Bachelor's degree in Computer Science from the University of California, Santa Barbara, and his Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.

* Long is currently a professor in the Baskin School of Engineering at the University of California, Santa Cruz, where he has been on the faculty since 1988.

* He served as the chair of the Computer Science Department at UC Santa Cruz from 2001 to 2004 and as the Associate Dean for Research in the Baskin School of Engineering from 2009 to 2015.

* Long's research focuses on data storage systems, including disk and tape storage, storage networks, and archival storage.

* He has made important contributions to the development of disk-based storage systems, including the invention of the first disk-based backup system.

* Long has also worked on the design and implementation of several storage systems, including the UCSD I/O Advisor and the CMU Tertiary Disk.

* He has collaborated with industry partners on several projects, including the development of a high-performance storage system for the San Diego Supercomputer Center.

* Long has published over 200 papers on data storage and computer systems and holds several patents in the field.

* He has received several awards for his contributions to the field of computer science, including the IEEE Computer Society Technical Committee on Storage Distinguished Service Award.

* Long has also served on the editorial boards of several journals, including the IEEE Transactions on Computers and the ACM Transactions on Storage.

* He is a Fellow of the IEEE and a member of the ACM.
Give a bullet point biography of Darrell Long highlighting their contributions and achievements as a computer scientist, with each fact separated with a new line character.

Here is a bullet point biography of Darrell Long:

* Darrell Long is a renowned computer scientist and professor of computer science.

* He received his Bachelor's degree in Computer Science from the University of California, Berkeley in 1986.

* Long earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of California, Berkeley in 1992.

* He is best known for his work on storage systems, data management, and cloud computing.

* Long has made significant contributions to the development of data deduplication, a technique used to eliminate duplicate data in storage systems.

* He has authored or co-authored over 200 papers in top-tier conferences and journals, including VLDB, SIGMOD, and ICDE.

* Long has served on the program committees of numerous conferences, including VLDB, SIGMOD, and ICDE.

* He is a Fellow of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) and a member of the ACM.

* Long has received several awards for his contributions to computer science, including the IEEE Computer Society Technical Committee on Data Engineering (TCDE) Rising Star Award.

* He has been a professor at the University of California, Santa Cruz since 1992, where he has supervised numerous Ph.D. students.

* Long's research has been recognized for its impact on the storage industry, and he has collaborated with several companies, including Google, Microsoft, and NetApp.

J\u00fcrg Gutknecht.content: Give a bullet point biography of Jürg Gutknecht highlighting their contributions and achievements as a computer scientist, with each fact separated with a new line character.

Here's a bullet point biography of Jürg Gutknecht:

* Jürg Gutknecht is a Swiss computer scientist.

* He studied computer science at ETH Zurich, where he received his Master's degree in 1981 and his Ph.D. in 1987.

* In 1988, he joined the IBM Zurich Research Laboratory.

* From 1997 to 2001, he was a research scientist at the Apple Zurich Research Laboratory.

* In 2001, he joined the ETH Zurich as a senior lecturer for Computer Science.

* He worked on various operating systems, particularly Oberon, a multi-programming operating system developed at ETH Zurich.

* He and Niklaus Wirth redesigned the Oberon operating system in 1991.

* He also worked on the Component Pascal programming language.

* He served on the steering committee of the IFIP Working Group 2.1 on Programming Languages and Their Applications.

* He led the FOSSGIS-konferenz series.

* He was actively involved in the development of the NetRiders competition.

* He focused on promoting free and open-source software.

* He was also involved in the development of the Modifiable Books research prototype.

* He passed away on 2 October 2021.
Give a bullet point biography of Jürg Gutknecht highlighting their contributions and achievements as a computer scientist, with each fact separated with a new line character.

Here's a bullet point biography of Jürg Gutknecht:

• Jürg Gutknecht is a Swiss computer scientist.

• He received his Master's degree in computer science from ETH Zurich.

• He then worked at IBM Zurich Research Laboratory.

• In 1986, he joined the Department of Computer Science at ETH Zurich as a lecturer.

• He became a full professor in 1991.

• He served as the president of the Swiss Federal Council's Information and Communication Technology Group.

• He developed the Oberon operating system.

• He was involved in the design and implementation of the Oberon System along with Niklaus Wirth.

• His contributions to computer science have been significant, particularly in the areas of operating systems and programming languages.
